summary: preinstall hook reads /flag.txt and exfiltrates contents to local HTTP services
details: Package declares `preinstall: node index.js` in package.json, causing index.js to execute automatically on `npm install`. The script reads `/flag.txt` from the installer's filesystem (falling back to `cat /flag*` via child_process.execSync), then issues HTTP PUT requests to 127.0.0.1 on ports 3000, 8080, 80, 1337, 8000, and 3001 with path `/api/modules/ECT-987654`, embedding the file contents in a JSON payload. Package metadata is generic placeholder content (description `Probe`, empty author, numeric-suffix name `ect-839201`, version `100.0.7`), consistent with CTF-style probe / red-team tooling rather than a legitimate utility. Any installer with a `/flag.txt` file and a local HTTP service listening on one of the targeted ports will have that file's contents transmitted on install.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
